quotes.text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: In the midst of movement and chaos, keep stillness inside of you.
quotes.author: Deepak Chopra

quotes.text: Quiet the mind, and the soul will speak.
quotes.author: Ma Jaya Sati Bhagavati

quotes.text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
quotes.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

quotes.text: Meditation is not about stopping thoughts, but recognizing that we are more than our thoughts.
quotes.author: Arianna Huffington

quotes.text: The soul always knows what to do to heal itself. The challenge is to silence the mind.
quotes.author: Caroline Myss

quotes.text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against your challenges.
quotes.author: Bryant McGill

quotes.text: Silence is not empty, it is full of answers.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Within you there is a stillness and a sanctuary to which you can retreat.
quotes.author: Hermann Hesse

quotes.text: The universe is not outside of you. Look inside yourself; everything that you want, you already are.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: When you realize there is nothing lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: The only way to make sense out of change is to plunge into it, move with it, and join the dance.
quotes.author: Alan Watts

quotes.text: What you seek is seeking you.
quotes.author: Rumi

dohas.text:
काल करे सो आज कर, आज करे सो अब ।
पल में प्रलय होयगी, बहुरि करेगा कब ॥
dohas.translation:
Do tomorrow's work today, and today's work right now.
If destruction comes in a moment, when will you get another chance?
dohas.meaning: This doha emphasizes the importance of not procrastinating and acting with urgency in spiritual matters.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
दुःख में सुमिरन सब करे, सुख में करे न कोय ।
जो सुख में सुमिरन करे, तो दुःख काहे को होय ॥
dohas.translation:
Everyone remembers God in sorrow, none in happiness.
If one remembers God in happiness, why would sorrow come?
dohas.meaning: Regular spiritual practice during good times prevents future suffering.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
गुरु गोविन्द दोऊ खड़े, काके लागूं पाँय ।
बलिहारी गुरु आपने, गोविन्द दियो मिलाय ॥
dohas.translation:
If Guru and God both stand before me, whose feet should I touch first?
I bow to my Guru, who showed me the path to God.
dohas.meaning: This highlights the supreme importance of the spiritual teacher in one's journey.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
बोली एक अनमोल है, जो कोई बोलै जानि ।
हिये तराजू तौलि के, तब मुख बाहर आनि ॥
dohas.translation:
Speech is priceless if one knows how to speak.
Weigh it on the scales of your heart before bringing it to your mouth.
dohas.meaning: Think before you speak; words have great power and should be used wisely.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
माला फेरत जुग भया, फिरा न मन का फेर ।
कर का मनका डार दे, मन का मनका फेर ॥
dohas.translation:
Ages have passed turning prayer beads, but the mind hasn't turned to God.
Drop the beads from your hands, and turn the beads of your mind instead.
dohas.meaning: External rituals are meaningless without internal transformation and devotion.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
पोथी पढ़ि पढ़ि जग मुआ, पंडित भया न कोय ।
ढाई आखर प्रेम का, पढ़े सो पंडित होय ॥
dohas.translation:
Reading books, the world died, but none became wise.
One who reads the two and a half letters of 'Love' becomes truly learned.
dohas.meaning: True wisdom comes from love and devotion, not just bookish knowledge.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
जाति न पूछो साधु की, पूछ लीजिये ज्ञान ।
मोल करो तरवार का, पड़ा रहन दो म्यान ॥
dohas.translation:
Don't ask about a saint's caste, ask about their knowledge.
Value the sword, let the sheath remain aside.
dohas.meaning: Judge people by their spiritual wisdom and character, not by their birth or social status.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
साईं इतना दीजिये, जा में कुटुम समाय ।
मैं भी भूखा न रहूं, साधु न भूखा जाय ॥
dohas.translation:
O Lord, give me just enough to sustain my family.
So that I don't go hungry, and no saint goes hungry from my door.
dohas.meaning: A prayer for moderation - enough to live with dignity and help others.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
निंदक नियरे राखिए, आंगन कुटी छवाय ।
बिन पानी, साबुन बिना, निर्मल करे सुभाय ॥
dohas.translation:
Keep your critic close, build them a hut in your courtyard.
Without water or soap, they'll clean your character.
dohas.meaning: Critics help us see our flaws and improve ourselves; welcome constructive criticism.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

dohas.text:
धीरे-धीरे रे मना, धीरे सब कुछ होय ।
माली सींचे सौ घड़ा, ऋतु आए फल होय ॥
dohas.translation:
Slowly, slowly, O mind, everything happens in its time.
The gardener may water a hundred pots, but fruit comes only in season.
dohas.meaning: Patience is essential in spiritual growth; results come at the right time with consistent effort.
dohas.author: Sant Kabir Das

affirmations.text: I am at peace with the flow of life.
I radiate love, compassion, and kindness.
I trust the divine plan unfolding in my life.
I am grateful for every blessing, seen and unseen.
My inner light guides me through all challenges.
I release all that no longer serves my highest good.
I am connected to the infinite wisdom within me.
Every breath fills me with peace and clarity.
I choose love over fear in every moment.
I am worthy of all the good that comes my way.

books.title: Bhagavad Gita
books.author: Vyasa
books.description: The timeless spiritual dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna on duty, righteousness, and the nature of reality.

books.title: Kabir Ke Dohe
books.author: Sant Kabir Das
books.description: Collection of spiritual couplets by Saint Kabir that illuminate the path to self-realization.

books.title: Ramayana
books.author: Maharishi Valmiki
books.description: The epic tale of Lord Rama's life, teachings, and the triumph of dharma over adharma.

books.title: The Power of Now
books.author: Eckhart Tolle
books.description: A guide to spiritual enlightenment that emphasizes the importance of living in the present moment.

books.title: Autobiography of a Yogi
books.author: Paramahansa Yogananda
books.description: A spiritual classic introducing Western readers to the teachings of meditation and Kriya Yoga.
